# Slider

Selects one or more numeric values by moving thumbs along a bounded track.

## Installation

Add Slider from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/slider
```

## Usage

Use Slider for quick relative adjustment within a known range. Its model is always a `number[]`, including a single-thumb slider. The local component renders one thumb for every model entry.

**Volume example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-3">
    <div class="flex items-center justify-between text-sm">
      <Label>Volume</Label>
      <span class="text-muted-foreground">{{ value[0] }}%</span>
    </div>
    <Slider v-model="value" :max="100" :step="1" />
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import { Slider } from '@/Components/Slider'

const value = ref([48])
</script>
```

## Range and steps

Set `min`, `max`, and `step` to match the domain. Defaults are 0, 100, and 1. For multiple thumbs, initialize the model with multiple sorted values and use `min-steps-between-thumbs` to enforce separation. The separation is measured in step units.

```vue
<Slider
  v-model="priceRange"
  :min="0"
  :max="500"
  :step="10"
  :min-steps-between-thumbs="2"
/>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
const priceRange = ref([100, 300])
</script>
```

## Controlled and committed values

Bind `v-model` when surrounding UI displays the current value and use `default-value` for uncontrolled initial state. The model updates continuously while dragging. Listen to `value-commit` for work that should run only after an interaction finishes, such as an expensive request or persisted preference.

## Orientation and direction

Horizontal is the default. For `orientation="vertical"`, give the containing layout an intentional height; the local styles provide a minimum but the final size still belongs to the page. Use `inverted` only when increasing in the opposite visual direction matches the domain, and let `dir` follow the surrounding reading direction.

## Keyboard and accessibility

Slider thumbs expose slider semantics and support arrow keys, Page Up, Page Down, Home, and End. Provide a visible label and a textual current value, because track position alone is not precise enough for every user. Multiple thumbs receive generated minimum/maximum labels; keep the range meaning obvious in nearby text.

## Validation and choosing a control

Set `name` when the slider participates in a form and disable it only when adjustment is unavailable. Validate the numeric array in application state, especially when one thumb depends on another. Avoid Slider for values users must enter exactly or compare digit by digit.

> **Use Number Field for exact entry: [Number Field](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/number-field)**
>
> Number Field supports typing, localized formatting, and explicit increment and decrement buttons for precise values.

Hosted documentation: [Slider](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/slider)
